channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Las empresas que no se adapten a la IA generativa podrían poner en jaque su negocio
item.description: Cuatro de cada diez directivos aproximadamente (44,1%) considera que aquellas organizaciones que no se adapten a la nueva era de la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa podría poner en jaque su negocio llegando, incluso, a condenarles a desaparecer.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Copilot Studio Agents get a major upgrade via Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 put an end to Microsoft’s dalliance with chatbots. Not because the company is walking away from natural language interfaces, but because it’s taking what it learned with its first Copilots and refocusing on tangible productivity gains from large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Orange acuerda con OpenAI el acceso a sus modelos de IA antes de su lanzamiento
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) ha ganado un protagonismo incontestable desde la irrupción de ChatGPT a manos de OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Swiss 'AI Jesus' project to bridge digital and the divine draws users' praise, as questions remain
item.description: LUCERNE, Switzerland (AP) — Would you trust an “AI Jesus” with your innermost thoughts and troubles? Researchers and religious leaders on Wednesday released findings from a two-month experiment through art in a Catholic chapel in Switzerland, where an avatar of “Jesus” on a computer screen — tucked
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Australian Senate debates social media ban for under-16s
item.description: The major parties' support for the ban all but guarantees the legislation will become law. But many child welfare and mental health advocates are concerned about unintended consequences.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Swipe right on data privacy: What tech companies can learn about consent from dating apps
item.description: I’ve been in the data privacy field for over 15 years, but nothing could prepare me for the complications and complexities that AI has created for companies since it hit the mainstream in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Ron De Jesus, Fast Company

item.title: Gov. Newsom sets himself up as a foil to Elon Musk and Donald Trump
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- As Elon Musk prepares to wield more power in Washington, the state where he built his fortune is girding for a fight.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: For Black Friday deals, these 3 sites are all you need
item.description: I’m in my mid-forties. Things are going gray in places and my hips hurt for no reason. And back in my day, Black Friday was one day: the Friday after Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Breaking Down Language Barriers: Apple Watch's New Translation Feature
item.description: Apple Watch has transformed into a powerful translation tool with the latest update in WatchOS 11, breaking down language barriers and enabling users to communicate naturally across languages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Python to C: What’s new in Cython 3.1
item.description: Cython is the most convenient way to bridge C and Python languages and tools in Python programs. Developers use it to write C extensions or wrap C libraries for use in Python. Now, a major new release is on the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Kotlin to lose scripting features
item.description: JetBrains plans to drop some scripting-related technologies from the Kotlin language, including REPL functionality, an obsolete API for Java scripting, the KotlinScriptMojo Maven plugin, and the kotlin-scripting-ide-services library for implementing code completion functionality.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal investigators in New York are seeking records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed this summer in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: This free time-zone conversion tool blows other clock apps out of the water
item.description: You’d think by this point in our lives, we’d have the whole time-zone-conversion thing figured out. It isn’t all that complicated, right?! And yet—well, trying to keep such calculations straight often feels like performing advanced algebra.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: 10 ways AI can make farmers’ jobs easier
item.description: For those who work the earth, AI can be an invaluable tool for making that work more efficient, sustainable and productive. AI helps farmers use the same tools they have been using for centuries, but with improved efficiency and focus.
item.dc:creator: Richard Baguley, Fast Company

item.title: Surveillance tech advances by Biden could aid in Trump's promised crackdown on immigration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration — among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Garance Burke And Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Backup Software vs. Backup as a Service (BaaS): The Key Differences
item.description: As data volumes increase exponentially, the threat of data loss from cyberattacks, human error, or system failure has never been greater — which is why, in 2025, fortifying a data protection strategy has never made more sense.
item.dc:creator: Network World
